task_completion.description: Verify that a clinical encounter or evaluation has been initiated for the patient John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GNU Health interface showing an active clinical encounter, evaluation, or patient record open for 'John Zenon'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the condition is documented as Toxic effect of carbon monoxide (ICD-10 T58).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The condition 'Toxic effect of carbon monoxide' or the ICD-10 code 'T58' is visible in the diagnosis or conditions section of the evaluation.

task_completion.description: Verify that the patient's tachycardia and tachypnea are documented with appropriate vital signs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the clinical evaluation's vital signs section, Heart Rate (HR) is recorded as 100 or higher, and Respiratory Rate (RR) is recorded as 20 or higher.

task_completion.description: Verify that an appropriate analgesic is prescribed for the headache.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A prescription or medication order for 'Paracetamol', 'Ibuprofen', or 'Acetaminophen' is visible in the patient's record.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least two baseline laboratory tests have been ordered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The lab requests or laboratory section shows at least two distinct lab tests ordered for the patient.

task_completion.description: Verify that a follow-up appointment is scheduled for 1 to 5 days from the current date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment is scheduled for John Zenon with a date that is 1 to 5 days after the system's current date.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GNU Health desktop client, not terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent entered data into the appropriate structured fields rather than dumping text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data such as HR, RR, condition codes, and prescriptions are entered into their respective dedicated fields, not just pasted as a single block of text into a notes field.

integrity.description: Verify that the actual GNU Health application is being used.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard GNU Health/Tryton interface is visible, with appropriate menus, tabs, and window titles.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is John Zenon. The required ICD-10 code is T58. Tachycardia requires a Heart Rate (HR) >= 100. Tachypnea requires a Respiratory Rate (RR) >= 20. Acceptable analgesics are Paracetamol, Ibuprofen, or Acetaminophen. At least 2 lab tests must be ordered. The follow-up appointment must be scheduled 1 to 5 days from the current system date.
